pr.title: items: match 19 functions across item files
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Merged item decomp PR that replaced 19 previously stubbed item functions with matching C implementations across many item files. The changes were mostly small item state, animation, physics, destruction, effect, and fighter-interaction callbacks, supported by item variable struct/layout fixes and typed header prototypes. The PR body and decomp-dev bot report both state all 19 functions reached 100% match; the bot reported +1740 matched bytes and matched code rising to 55.82%.

key_files.role: Adjusted shared item variable layouts: added itLipstickSpore_ItemVars, added Tincle x4C, made Pokemon xE44 a f32/s32 union, and typed Oldottosea x24.
Added lipstickspore to the Item xDD4_itemVar union.
Matched it_802AFEA8 dispatcher for thrown needle behavior.
Matched it_802C573C, including efLib_DestroyAll and item cleanup.
Matched it_2725_Logic90_Destroyed with Popo/Kirby callback handoff.
Matched it_2725_Logic39_Destroyed using Pikachu thunder state checks.
Matched it_802CA8DC using a flag, helper call, velocity update, and M2C_FIELD offset access.
Matched it_802E2E30 velocity reset and motion selection.
Matched itTincle_UnkMotion3_Phys using newly exposed x4C field.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed signatures for it_802CA014 and it_802CA3F4.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with typed signatures for it_802CA8DC and it_802CAB10.
Typed helper prototypes it_802AFF08 and it_802B00F4.

what_changed: Decompiled 19 item functions listed in the PR body: it_802C8C34, it_2725_Logic80_PickedUp, it_802AFEA8, it_802C573C, itFlipper_UnkMotion5_Anim, itFlipper_UnkMotion6_Anim, it_802E31F8, it_2725_Logic39_Destroyed, it_2725_Logic90_Destroyed, itPatapata_UnkMotion7_Anim, it_802D2A58, it_802B3F20, it_802BFE5C, it_802CB844, it_802CA8DC, it_802E2E30, itTincle_UnkMotion3_Phys, it_802CA014, and itLipstickspore_UnkMotion0_Phys.

Implemented multiple animation-state transitions through Item_80268E5C and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, including Clinkmilk picked-up motion selection by facing_dir, Flipper motion 5/6 toggles based on xDE4, Oldottosea motion 6/7 selection, and Whitebea motion 8 transition.

Implemented cleanup/destruction helpers such as Clinkmilk clearing owner and item vars before Item_8026A8EC, Mewtwo Shadow Ball calling efLib_DestroyAll and clearing x28, Climbers Ice notifying Popo/Kirby fighter code before clearing its owner pointer, and Pikachu Thunder restoring fighter state when conditions match.

Implemented small physics/state helpers: Lipstickspore horizontal velocity damping, Tincle vertical velocity/countdown behavior, Matadogas velocity scaling by item kind attributes, Kamex flagged velocity update from offset 0xE3C, Ness Yoyo link velocity setup, and Pikachu Tjoltair bone-position extraction.

Added or corrected item variable layout data needed by the matches: itLipstickSpore_ItemVars with xDD4/xDD8, Tincle x4C, Pokemon xE44_s32 alias, Oldottosea x24, and the lipstickspore union member in Item.
Updated several item headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete typed prototypes, especially for Flipper, Kabigon, Kamex, Seak Needle Thrown, Oldottosea, and Tincle helpers.
Added includes for dependencies used by the new code: ef/eflib.h, ftKb_Init.h, ftPp_Init.h, ftPk_SpecialLw.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, it/inlines.h, it/item.h, and itCommonItems.h.
smart_moves: Matched code and data layout together instead of only filling function bodies; the new functions depended on precise xDD4 item-var fields and header prototype cleanup.
Used a union for itPokemon_ItemVars.xE44 so Kabigon could decrement the same offset as an s32 timer while preserving the existing f32 interpretation for other users.
Kept unknown or offset-derived fields named conservatively, such as xDD8, x4C, x24, and xE44_s32, avoiding invented semantic names where the PR provided no evidence.

Used existing item/fighter/effect helper APIs rather than open-coding behavior: Item_80268E5C, Item_802694CC, Item_8026A8EC, efLib_DestroyAll, lb_8000B1CC, ftPp_Init_8011F16C, ftKb_SpecialNIc_80108CE8, and ftPk_SpecialLw helpers.
Preserved exact-looking branch shapes and null checks from the target functions, including nested gobj/user_data checks, owner_gobj checks, and conditional callback assignment.
Used narrow prototype improvements in headers to unlock cross-file calls while leaving still-unknown functions as unknown where evidence was not added.
decomp_lessons: Small item functions often become matchable once the correct item-var substruct fields are exposed in itCommonItems.h and wired into Item.xDD4_itemVar.

When one item-var offset is used with different types across contexts, a union alias can be a safer matching step than forcing one interpretation; this PR demonstrates that with Pokemon xE44/xE44_s32.
Header cleanup is part of matching work: replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with concrete Item_GObj*, Fighter_GObj*, Vec3*, bool, and void signatures enabled or documented the new implementations.
Do not infer semantic field names prematurely. The PR left many offset-style names intact while still improving type information enough to match.
Stack-shape details still matter in short callbacks; the diff uses PAD_STACK(8) in itPatapata_UnkMotion7_Anim and it_802B3F20.

Normal C style may diverge from matching needs: the diff includes non-void/boolean-looking callbacks with paths lacking explicit return values, and the PR still reported 100% function matches and a clean ninja build.
naming_conventions: Known item motion callbacks follow it<Item>_UnkMotion<N>_(Anim|Phys|Coll), as in itFlipper_UnkMotion5_Anim and itTincle_UnkMotion3_Phys.
Unidentified functions remain address-based with the it_802xxxxx form, such as it_802CA014, it_802CB844, and it_802E2E30.
Logic callbacks use it_2725_LogicNN_<Event>, such as it_2725_Logic80_PickedUp, it_2725_Logic90_Destroyed, and it_2725_Logic39_Destroyed.
Item variable structs use it<Item>_ItemVars naming, e.g. itLipstickSpore_ItemVars and itOldottosea_ItemVars.
Unknown fields keep offset-style names and type suffixes when useful, e.g. xDD4, xDD8, x4C, x24, and xE44_s32.
Header prototypes retain address comments while replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS with typed signatures.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added PAD_STACK(8) where needed for stack matching in itPatapata_UnkMotion7_Anim and it_802B3F20.
Used M2C_FIELD(ip, f32*, 0xE3C) in it_802CA8DC rather than expanding the Item struct for a single uncertain Kamex offset.
Manually implemented absolute-value logic in itLipstickspore_UnkMotion0_Phys instead of using a library fabs call, matching the visible control flow.
Matched float comparisons and constants directly, such as facing_dir == 1.0f, xDE4 != 0.0f, xDE4 == 0.0F, and zeroing Vec3 components explicitly.
Used typed callback casts where needed, such as assigning it_802D2B4C through (HSD_GObjEvent) in it_802D2A58.
Kept early-return/null-check control flow in short dispatcher/cleanup functions, especially it_802AFEA8 and it_802C573C.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported GALE01 matched code 55.82% (+0.04%, +1740 bytes) with 19 new matches, each moving from 0.00% to 100.00%.
Also reported .sdata2 improvements for itnessyoyo (+7 bytes, 13.33%) and itclinkmilk (+5 bytes, 66.67%).
No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type itLipstickSpore_ItemVars.xDD8, itTincle_ItemVars.x4C, itOldottosea_ItemVars.x24, or itPokemon_ItemVars.xE44_s32.
Search remaining item headers for UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS near the helper functions touched by this PR.
Check whether Kamex offset M2C_FIELD(ip, f32*, 0xE3C) was later moved into a typed item-var or attribute struct.
Search for later cleanup or discussion of non-void callbacks without explicit returns, especially it_802AFEA8 and itFlipper_UnkMotion6_Anim.
Compare later item PRs that use PAD_STACK(8) in short item animation or position helper functions.
